summary: The functional test `test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` failed because pip could not download a package from GitHub due to repeated HTTP 502 errors. The test was retried 3 times and failed each time, indicating a transient network issue.
root_cause: The test attempted to install a package from `https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip` but GitHub returned too many 502 errors, causing pip to fail with `HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))`.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: This is the actual error from pip showing that GitHub returned repeated 502 errors, a transient network failure.
The test was rerun 3 times (shown 3 RERUN entries), confirming it was treated as a flaky/network failure and retried.
Final test failure summary confirming this was the only failing test.
Shows the test was making a real network request to GitHub, which is susceptible to transient failures.

suggested_fix: This is a transient network failure (GitHub 502 errors). Re-running the CI job should resolve it. If it recurs frequently, consider mocking the network request or using a more stable package source for the test.
